reasoning: Recent data for TMBACBW027SBOG shows weekly MBS held by commercial banks has risen strongly throughout 2025, from around 2626 billion in January to 2720 billion in late October. The average positive weekly drift is approximately +2.3 billion, against an estimated weekly standard deviation of 7.3 billion. Based on this short-term positive momentum, I estimate a ~63% chance of seeing an increase within the 1-week horizon (2025-11-02). However, looking at the entire 1-year window provided, there was a period of decline from October 2024 into January 2025 (falling roughly 25 billion), indicating the potential for multi-month negative trends or regime shifts. This structural dynamic means longer forecasting horizons face an increasing risk of a trend reversal. Following the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase progressively with horizon, the medium-term (1-month, 2025-11-25) and longer-term (3-month, 2026-01-24) probabilities are increasingly penalized toward 0.5, assigned as 57% and 54% respectively.
